  • 30 Popular Cat Breeds In India (Updated)

30 Popular Cat Breeds In India (Updated)

By HUFT Editorial

Oh, to come home to affectionate purrs, four fuzzy paws and curious little whiskers—is that your dream life? It sounds like you might be looking to welcome a cat in your life! 

Cats have a special way of turning a house into a home with their playful antics, independent spirit, and quiet companionship. If you are thinking about welcoming a feline friend into your life, get ready for endless cuddles, quirky surprises, and a bond like no other. Here is a list of the most popular cat breeds in India to help you make an informed decision!

1. Spotted Cat (Indian Billi)

The Indian Billi or Spotted Cats are the most common cat breeds from India. This Indian cat breed has, over years of living as community street cats, evolved to be independent, active and inquisitive. They are also low-maintenance and don’t need an intricate grooming routine. Win their trust and watch them become loving and loyal friends forever! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 18 years 
  • Origin: India

2. Bengal Cat

Descendents of the Wild Asian Leopard Cat, Bengal Cats are one of the most intelligent and elegant domestic cat breeds in India. They are known to be very talkative and expressive. They also need a LOT of exercise, so keep up with their energy levels—build a play-area for them, take them out for walks, and get interactive cat toys! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 16 years
  • Origin: India

3. Bombay Cat

Bombay Cats are one of the most affectionate and smart small cat breeds—it’s adorable to see how confidently they demand pets! With their sleek black coats and striking eyes, they are beautiful, like mini Black Panthers. They require minimum grooming and happily adapt to homes of any size. It’s super easy to train them too, with a few yummy cat treats! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 13 years
  • Origin: United States

4. Maine Coon

Maine Coons are super friendly, sociable and lovingly called ‘gentle giants’. They can grow up to 10 kg and 40 inches in size. They adapt to domestic living easily, and are a great choice for families with children. However, due to their longer coat, it is important to keep them cool, well-groomed and hydrated in summer. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 12 - 15 years
  • Origin: United States

5. Persian Cat

Persian Cats are one of the cutest cat breeds out there! They have a gorgeous coat, beautiful eyes and chubby cheeks. They are lovely indoor cats who love watching the world go by. They want to be the centre of your attention, but are equally content in their own company. Be prepared to groom and pamper them daily! 

  • Not ideal for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 17 years
  • Origin: Iran

6. Siamese Cat

Out of all the types of cat breeds, Siamese Cats have historically been the most popular pets! They are independent, sociable, loyal and very expressive. Their confident, loud meows will echo through your home. Fun fact: the colour of their coat changes with external temperatures! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 12 years
  • Origin: Thailand

7. Himalayan Cat

Himalayan Cats are gentle and quiet balls of fur who make excellent indoor pets. They love a good cuddle session, but are also happy playing and keeping themselves entertained. However, they are notoriously fussy eaters and prone to health troubles. Understand their diets, and invest in quality cat food. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 15 years
  • Origin: United States

8. Abyssinian

If you are looking for good cat breeds who are friendly with children as well as other cats or pets, the Abyssinian is your perfect companion! They are adored for their playful and inquisitive spirits, and short, low-maintenance coats. Just keep them active and engaged with stimulating cat toys! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 15 years
  • Origin: Ethiopia

9. Sphynx Cat

Hairless and wrinkly, Sphynx Cats are unique cat breeds. Contrary to popular opinion, they actually have quite silly personalities, and form very strong, affectionate bonds with their humans! They just need some extra warmth during the colder months, and regular baths all-season to prevent oil build-up on the skin. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 6 - 18 years
  • Origin: Canada

10. American Bobtail

American Bobtails have unique traits—a distinctively short tail, muscular build and a highly intelligent mind. Promise them lots of cat treats and active playtime, and they will adore you! Be mindful of scheduling regular grooming sessions to keep their thick, double coat in pristine condition. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 13 - 18 years
  • Origin: United States

11. Birman Cat

Birman cats are truly majestic, not just in their striking appearance but also in their rich history, they were once considered sacred in Burmese temples. With their affectionate nature and easygoing temperament, they make wonderful companions. They are relatively low-maintenance and get along well with children, making them an ideal choice for first-time cat parents.

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 15 years
  • Origin: Myanmar

12. Oriental Shorthair

There are many different types of cat breeds, but the Oriental Shorthair stands out with a sleek and elegant appearance. They are active and intelligent, and very vocal about their wants and needs—so make sure you keep them engaged and entertained! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 15 years
  • Origin: Thailand/United States

13. Turkish Van

Turkish Van cats are a medium to large cat breed, known for their playful nature and a lush, low-maintenance white coat. Unlike others, most Turkish Vans love water! They enjoy playing in shallow pools or with running faucets. Shower them with love, and they will form strong bonds with every family member! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 12 - 17 years
  • Origin: Türkiye

14. Ragdoll Cat

True to their name, Ragdoll Cats have adorable, doll-like features and silky coats—one of the cutest cat breeds out there! You will find these gentle cats spending most of their time calmly curled up on the floor, or following their favourite human like a shadow! 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 12 - 15 years
  • Origin: United States

15. Tonkinese Cat

If you welcome a Tonkinese Cat home, be prepared to make them the centre of attention! It will be effortless though, because it is tough to not fall in love with their affectionate, playful spirits. They will learn tricks super fast, but need a proper diet and good amounts of exercise. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 12 - 15 years
  • Origin: Canada

16. British Shorthair

The British Shorthair is a calm and affectionate breed, often described as a cuddly ball of fur. Their round faces, plush coats, and gentle demeanor make them one of the most adorable companions. Friendly and easygoing, they adapt well to indoor living, but their dense fur can make them prone to overheating. Keeping them cool, comfortable, and well-hydrated ensures they stay happy and healthy.

  • Not ideal for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 12 - 20 years
  • Origin: United Kingdom

17. Scottish Fold

Scottish Fold cats are one of the most calm, gentle and loving domestic cat breeds. They are easy to live with and care for. They get their name from their unique ears, that bend or ‘fold’ forwards—making for a very expressive, adorable face! 

  • Not ideal for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 14 - 16 years
  • Origin: Scotland

18. Devon Rex

The Devon Rex is a playful and energetic cat breed known for its mischievous nature and love for adventure. These cats enjoy climbing, jumping, and even playing fetch, making them an entertaining companion. With their large ears, expressive eyes, and soft, wavy coat, they have a unique and charming appearance. 

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 9 - 15 years
  • Origin: England

19. Egyptian Mau

The Egyptian Mau is known to be intelligent, energetic and muscular. Their remarkably spotted, low-maintenance coat and strikingly pretty eyes make them picture-perfect pets. Be sure to buy them lots of cat toys that encourage and stimulate their natural hunting instincts.

  • Suitable for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 13 - 16 years
  • Origin: Egypt

20. Russian Blue

The Russian Blue cat is known for a beautiful bluish-grey coat that is short, but dense and dual layered. So, they need regular grooming. You will find in them a loyal, calm yet curious companion for life! 

  • Not ideal for Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 20 years
  • Origin: Russia

21. Khao Manee

This rare Thai breed is known for its stunning white coat and striking eyes, often of different colours. Khao Manees are highly social and affectionate, making them a great choice for families. They are also quite vocal and expressive.

  • Suitable for the Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 10 - 12 years
  • Origin: Thailand

22. Balinese

Balinese cats are closely related to Siamese but with a luxurious, silky coat. They are known for their vocal and loving nature. These intelligent cats love to play and interact with their humans, making them ideal for indoor living.

  • Not suitable for the Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 18-22 years
  • Origin: United States

23. Cornish Rex

Cornish Rex cats are unique due to their wavy, soft coat and slender, athletic body. They are playful, curious, and highly active. They need plenty of toys and space to explore and thrive in loving homes.

  • Suitable for the Indian climate
  • Life expectancy: 11 - 14 years
  • Origin: England

    24. Singapura

    One of the smallest cat breeds, Singapura cats are full of charm and energy. They have a sleek coat and a friendly, social nature, making them perfect companions. Their playful antics will keep you entertained for hours!

    • Suitable for the Indian climate
    • Life expectancy: 11 - 15 years
    • Origin: Singapore

    25. Manx

    Manx cats are unique because they are tailless! They are playful and intelligent cats that enjoy interacting with their humans. Their strong hind legs make them great jumpers, and they thrive in homes that provide mental stimulation.

    • Suitable for the Indian climate
    • Life expectancy: 14 - 16 years
    • Origin: Isle of Man

    26. Japanese Bobtail

    Full of charm and spirit, this cat is famous for its unique pom-pom tail. These cats are often associated with good luck in Japanese culture, making it even more special. With a lively and playful nature, Japanese Bobtail is always up for a game or a cuddle. If you’re looking for a chatty companion, this one won’t disappoint!

    • Suitable for the Indian Climate

    • Life expectancy: 15 - 18 years

    • Origin: Japan

    27. Turkish Angora

    Elegant and full of personality, this beauty moves like it’s on a catwalk. Its soft, silky coat and striking eyes instantly steal the spotlight. Behind that graceful look is a playful, intelligent cat that loves attention. It’s the perfect mix of class and sass.

    • Not ideal for the Indian climate

    • Life expectancy: 15 - 20 years

    • Origin: Türkiye

    28. LaPerm

    Curly fur and a quirky vibe make this kitty stand out from the crowd. Friendly, affectionate, and full of love, it enjoys being around people. Those springy curls aren’t just for looks; they match its bouncy personality, too! It’s like having a living soft toy at home.

    • Suitable for the Indian Climate

    • Life expectancy: 12 - 15 years

    • Origin: United States

    29. American Curl

    With ears that curl back like tiny waves, this cat has a look you’ll never forget. It’s affectionate, gentle, and adapts easily to different lifestyles. Always curious and kitten-like, even as it grows older. A cuddly, playful addition to any family.

    • Suitable for the Indian Climate

    • Life expectancy: 12 - 16 years

    • Origin: United States

    30. Chartreux

    With its plush blue-grey coat and soulful copper eyes, this cat is pure elegance without the fuss. Calm, quiet, and incredibly affectionate, it forms strong bonds with its humans. Despite its muscular build, it moves with surprising grace and agility. Low-maintenance and sweet-natured, it's a gentle companion who simply loves being nearby.

    • Not ideal for the Indian climate

    • Life expectancy: 12 - 15 years

    • Origin: France

    If you are thinking of adopting a cat, check out animal shelters and nearby NGOs. That way, not only do you give abandoned cats a loving home, but you don't have to break the bank to buy a costly breed! If you have just welcomed a new furry one in your home, search “pet store near me” and get them the best welcome-home-gifts!

    Frequently Asked Questions

    1. Which cat is best for a house?

    The best cat for your house depends on your lifestyle and schedule. Consider a cat’s average energy level, diet, grooming needs and temperament before making a decision.

    2. Which cat breed is lucky for home?

    Many cat breeds like the Sphynx, Persian and Birman are believed to bring good luck to the homes where they reside. However, every cat is special and promises to bring a lot of love, joy and high spirits to you!

    3. Which cat breed is most friendly?

    Ragdolls, Persians, Maine Coons and Absyssinians are usually super friendly, with humans and pets. However, factors like weather, living conditions, diet and more can affect their temperament. Be sure to care for them as they need.

    4. Which cat is more friendly, male or female?

    The gender of a cat does not affect their temperament. Males and females might have unique needs of their own, especially in heat, but both make loving companions. It is best to not have gender biases, and let your fur-ever friend choose you!

    5. How many cat breeds are there in India?

    Close to 15 unique cat breeds are known to naturally live in the Indian subcontinent, and many more as pets in Indian households.
